Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:\n\n## VS Code Diagnostics Pipeline - Complete Architecture Overview\n\nBased on my exploration of the VS Code codebase, here's a comprehensive guide to the diagnostics pipeline, file system handling, and related components:\n\n---\n\n### **1. DIAGNOSTICS PIPELINE**\n\n#### **Extension Host Diagnostics (Client Side)**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHostDiagnostics.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Manages diagnostic collections created by extensions. This is where extensions set and update diagnostics.\n\n**Key Components:**\n- **DiagnosticCollection class**: Stores diagnostics for each URI, with limits on diagnostics per file (1000) and total diagnostics (1100). Converts vscode diagnostics to IMarkerData format.\n- **ExtHostDiagnostics class**: Main service that manages all diagnostic collections and provides the public API. Uses a DebounceEmitter (50ms debounce) to batch diagnostic changes before sending to main thread.\n- **Flow**: Extension → DiagnosticCollection.set() → Converts to IMarkerData → Calls proxy.$changeMany() → Sends to MainThreadDiagnostics\n\n---\n\n#### **Main Thread Diagnostics (Main Side)**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Receives diagnostics from extensions and forwards them to the MarkerService on the main thread.\n\n**Key Components:**\n- Receives $changeMany() and $clear() calls from ExtHostDiagnostics\n- Revives URIs and validates marker data (relatedInformation, code targets)\n- Registers markers with IMarkerService via changeOne()\n- Tracks active diagnostic owners to prevent circular updates\n- Listens to IMarkerService.onMarkerChanged to forward external markers back to extension host via $acceptMarkersChange()\n\n---\n\n#### **Protocol Definition**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHost.protocol.ts`\n\n**Key Interfaces:**\n- **MainThreadDiagnosticsShape**: Defines RPC methods\n  - `$changeMany(owner: string, entries: [UriComponents, IMarkerData[]][]): void` - Set markers for specific files\n  - `$clear(owner: string): void` - Clear all markers for an owner\n  \n- **ExtHostDiagnosticsShape**: Defines reverse RPC\n  - `$acceptMarkersChange(data: [UriComponents, IMarkerData[]][]): void` - Accept external marker changes\n\n---\n\n### **2. MARKER SERVICE (Central Storage)**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/platform/markers/common/markerService.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Central repository for all markers/diagnostics in the system (both from extensions and other sources).\n\n**Key Components:**\n- **DoubleResourceMap**: Dual-indexed storage (by resource and by owner) for efficient lookups\n- **MarkerStats**: Tracks aggregate statistics (error/warning/info counts) and listens to marker changes\n- **Event Flow**: changeOne() → fire onMarkerChanged with affected URIs → listeners update\n\n**Key Methods:**\n- `changeOne(owner, resource, markerData)`: Update markers for a specific file\n- `changeAll(owner, data)`: Replace all markers for an owner\n- `read(filter)`: Query markers with filtering options (by owner, resource, severity)\n- `onMarkerChanged: Event<readonly URI[]>`: Fires when any markers change\n\n---\n\n### **3. FILE SYSTEM CHANGE HANDLING**\n\n#### **File Service (Integration Point)**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/platform/files/common/fileService.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Coordinates file system providers and their change events.\n\n**Key Components:**\n- `registerProvider()`: Registers file system providers for different schemes (file://, vscode://, etc.)\n- Forwards provider's `onDidChangeFile` events to two internal emitters:\n  - `internalOnDidFilesChange`: All events (with correlation tracking)\n  - `_onDidUncorrelatedFilesChange`: Public `onDidFilesChange` event (filtered to exclude correlated events)\n- Supports correlation IDs to track related file operations together\n\n**Event Architecture:**\n```\nFileSystemProvider.onDidChangeFile\n  ↓\nFileService.registerProvider()\n  ├→ internalOnDidFilesChange.fire(event)  [ALL events]\n  └→ onDidFilesChange.fire(event)         [Only non-correlated]\n```\n\n---\n\n#### **Base Watcher (Node.js Implementation)**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/platform/files/node/watcher/baseWatcher.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Abstract base class for file system watchers with suspend/resume capabilities.\n\n**Key Components:**\n- Manages watch requests with optional correlation IDs\n- Supports recursive and non-recursive watches with glob patterns\n- `watch(requests)`: Set up watchers for multiple paths\n- `onDidChangeFile: Event<IFileChange[]>`: Emits when files change\n- Handles watcher failures gracefully by suspending and retrying\n\n**Watch Request Structure:**\n- `path`: Directory to watch\n- `recursive`: Whether to watch subdirectories\n- `excludes`: Glob patterns to exclude\n- `includes`: Optional glob patterns to include\n- `correlationId`: Optional ID to group related changes\n- `filter`: Optional FileChangeFilter to filter event types\n\n---\n\n#### **Watcher Interface**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/platform/files/common/watcher.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Defines the contract for file watchers.\n\n**Key Interfaces:**\n- **IWatcher**: Core watcher interface\n  - `watch(requests: IUniversalWatchRequest[]): Promise<void>`\n  - `onDidChangeFile: Event<IFileChange[]>`\n  - `onDidLogMessage: Event<ILogMessage>`\n\n- **IUniversalWatchRequest**: Union of recursive and non-recursive watch requests\n- **IFileChange**: Represents a file system change with URI, type (added/updated/deleted), and optional correlationId\n\n---\n\n### **4. PROBLEMS VIEW (MARKERS VIEW)**\n\n#### **Markers View**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersView.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** The Problems panel UI that displays diagnostics to users.\n\n**How it Subscribes to Diagnostics:**\n1. Injects `IMarkerService` dependency\n2. On render, calls `markerService.read()` to get initial markers\n3. Subscribes to `markerService.onMarkerChanged` event\n4. Uses Event.debounce() with ResourceMap to batch updates before updating the MarkersModel\n5. Updates occur for specific resources that changed, not the entire list\n\n**Key Data Flow:**\n```\nDiagnostic Changes\n  ↓\nMarkerService.onMarkerChanged\n  ↓\nMarkersView.onMarkerChanged (debounced)\n  ↓\nmarkerService.read({ severities: Error|Warning|Info })\n  ↓\nMarkersModel.setResourceMarkers()\n  ↓\nUI Update (tree/table widget)\n```\n\n**Configuration Integration:**\n- Listens to configuration changes for `files.exclude` setting\n- Updates filter when exclusions change\n- Supports filtering by severity, text search, and active file\n\n---\n\n#### **Markers Model**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersModel.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Data model that transforms raw IMarker data into hierarchical ResourceMarkers structure.\n\n**Key Components:**\n- **ResourceMarkers**: Groups markers by file URI\n- **Marker**: Individual diagnostic with related information\n- **RelatedInformation**: Secondary diagnostic locations\n- **setResourceMarkers()**: Updates the model with new marker data, computes diffs (added/removed/updated), and fires onDidChange event\n\n**Cache Management:**\n- Caches sorted resource list for performance\n- Clears cache on any update\n- Lazy-loads line splitting for marker messages\n\n---\n\n#### **Tree/Table Rendering**\n**Files:**\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersTreeViewer.ts` - Tree structure renderer\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersTable.ts` - Table structure renderer\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersFilterOptions.ts` - Filter UI\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersFileDecorations.ts` - File decorations\n\n**Contribution:**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markers.contribution.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Registers the Problems view as a workbench panel and contributions.\n\n---\n\n#### **Markers Types & Constants**\n**Files:**\n- `/workspace/src/vs/platform/markers/common/markers.ts` - Public interfaces (IMarkerService, IMarkerData, MarkerSeverity)\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/common/markers.ts` - View-specific constants\n\n---\n\n### **5. KEY PATTERNS & INSIGHTS**\n\n1. **Debouncing**: Both diagnostic changes (50ms) and marker service events use debouncing to batch updates efficiently\n\n2. **Event-Driven**: The entire system is event-driven with clear separation of concerns via service interfaces\n\n3. **Correlation IDs**: File watcher and marker services use correlation IDs to track related operations together\n\n4. **Dual Indexing**: MarkerService uses DoubleResourceMap to index by both resource and owner for efficient lookups\n\n5. **RPC Protocol**: Clear request/response separation between extension host and main thread with well-defined shapes\n\n6. **Filtering**: Multiple layers of filtering (unsupported schemas, file.exclude, severity levels, search text)\n\n7. **URI Normalization**: Extensive use of extUri for case-sensitive/insensitive comparisons across platforms\n\nThis architecture enables VS Code to efficiently handle thousands of diagnostics from multiple extensions while maintaining responsiveness through intelligent batching and filtering."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a5ac75f2a103ff36e (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 55980\ntool_uses: 36\nduration_ms: 57171</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6badf5fd-141d-4b6c-ad07-2a2622d95059","uuid":"c9545538-3576-4521-bdef-4bd88c67c6c2","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Explore the VS Code codebase to understand:\n1. Converts vscode diagnostics to IMarkerData format.\n- **ExtHostDiagnostics class**: Main service that manages all diagnostic collections and provides the public API. Uses a DebounceEmitter (50ms debounce) to batch diagnostic changes before sending to main thread.\n- **Flow**: Extension → DiagnosticCollection.set() → Converts to IMarkerData → Calls proxy.$changeMany() → Sends to MainThreadDiagnostics\n\n---\n\n#### **Main Thread Diagnostics (Main Side)**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Receives diagnostics from extensions and forwards them to the MarkerService on the main thread.\n\n**Key Components:**\n- Receives $changeMany() and $clear() calls from ExtHostDiagnostics\n- Revives URIs and validates marker data (relatedInformation, code targets)\n- Registers markers with IMarkerService via changeOne()\n- Tracks active diagnostic owners to prevent circular updates\n- Listens to IMarkerService.onMarkerChanged to forward external markers back to extension host via $acceptMarkersChange()\n\n---\n\n#### **Protocol Definition**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/common/extHost.protocol.ts`\n\n**Key Interfaces:**\n- **MainThreadDiagnosticsShape**: Defines RPC methods\n  - `$changeMany(owner: string, entries: [UriComponents, IMarkerData[]][]): void` - Set markers for specific files\n  - `$clear(owner: string): void` - Clear all markers for an owner\n  \n- **ExtHostDiagnosticsShape**: Defines reverse RPC\n  - `$acceptMarkersChange(data: [UriComponents, IMarkerData[]][]): void` - Accept external marker changes\n\n---\n\n### **2. MARKER SERVICE (Central Storage)**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/platform/markers/common/markerService.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Central repository for all markers/diagnostics in the system (both from extensions and other sources).\n\n**Key Components:**\n- **DoubleResourceMap**: Dual-indexed storage (by resource and by owner) for efficient lookups\n- **MarkerStats**: Tracks aggregate statistics (error/warning/info counts) and listens to marker changes\n- **Event Flow**: changeOne() → fire onMarkerChanged with affected URIs → listeners update\n\n**Key Methods:**\n- `changeOne(owner, resource, markerData)`: Update markers for a specific file\n- `changeAll(owner, data)`: Replace all markers for an owner\n- `read(filter)`: Query markers with filtering options (by owner, resource, severity)\n- `onMarkerChanged: Event<readonly URI[]>`: Fires when any markers change\n\n---\n\n### **3. FILE SYSTEM CHANGE HANDLING**\n\n#### **File Service (Integration Point)**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/platform/files/common/fileService.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Coordinates file system providers and their change events.\n\n**Key Components:**\n- `registerProvider()`: Registers file system providers for different schemes (file://, vscode://, etc.)\n- Forwards provider's `onDidChangeFile` events to two internal emitters:\n  - `internalOnDidFilesChange`: All events (with correlation tracking)\n  - `_onDidUncorrelatedFilesChange`: Public `onDidFilesChange` event (filtered to exclude correlated events)\n- Supports correlation IDs to track related file operations together\n\n**Event Architecture:**\n```\nFileSystemProvider.onDidChangeFile\n  ↓\nFileService.registerProvider()\n  ├→ internalOnDidFilesChange.fire(event)  [ALL events]\n  └→ onDidFilesChange.fire(event)         [Only non-correlated]\n```\n\n---\n\n#### **Base Watcher (Node.js Implementation)**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/platform/files/node/watcher/baseWatcher.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Abstract base class for file system watchers with suspend/resume capabilities.\n\n**Key Components:**\n- Manages watch requests with optional correlation IDs\n- Supports recursive and non-recursive watches with glob patterns\n- `watch(requests)`: Set up watchers for multiple paths\n- `onDidChangeFile: Event<IFileChange[]>`: Emits when files change\n- Handles watcher failures gracefully by suspending and retrying\n\n**Watch Request Structure:**\n- `path`: Directory to watch\n- `recursive`: Whether to watch subdirectories\n- `excludes`: Glob patterns to exclude\n- `includes`: Optional glob patterns to include\n- `correlationId`: Optional ID to group related changes\n- `filter`: Optional FileChangeFilter to filter event types\n\n---\n\n#### **Watcher Interface**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/platform/files/common/watcher.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Defines the contract for file watchers.\n\n**Key Interfaces:**\n- **IWatcher**: Core watcher interface\n  - `watch(requests: IUniversalWatchRequest[]): Promise<void>`\n  - `onDidChangeFile: Event<IFileChange[]>`\n  - `onDidLogMessage: Event<ILogMessage>`\n\n- **IUniversalWatchRequest**: Union of recursive and non-recursive watch requests\n- **IFileChange**: Represents a file system change with URI, type (added/updated/deleted), and optional correlationId\n\n---\n\n### **4. PROBLEMS VIEW (MARKERS VIEW)**\n\n#### **Markers View**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersView.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** The Problems panel UI that displays diagnostics to users.\n\n**How it Subscribes to Diagnostics:**\n1. Injects `IMarkerService` dependency\n2. On render, calls `markerService.read()` to get initial markers\n3. Subscribes to `markerService.onMarkerChanged` event\n4. Uses Event.debounce() with ResourceMap to batch updates before updating the MarkersModel\n5. Updates occur for specific resources that changed, not the entire list\n\n**Key Data Flow:**\n```\nDiagnostic Changes\n  ↓\nMarkerService.onMarkerChanged\n  ↓\nMarkersView.onMarkerChanged (debounced)\n  ↓\nmarkerService.read({ severities: Error|Warning|Info })\n  ↓\nMarkersModel.setResourceMarkers()\n  ↓\nUI Update (tree/table widget)\n```\n\n**Configuration Integration:**\n- Listens to configuration changes for `files.exclude` setting\n- Updates filter when exclusions change\n- Supports filtering by severity, text search, and active file\n\n---\n\n#### **Markers Model**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersModel.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Data model that transforms raw IMarker data into hierarchical ResourceMarkers structure.\n\n**Key Components:**\n- **ResourceMarkers**: Groups markers by file URI\n- **Marker**: Individual diagnostic with related information\n- **RelatedInformation**: Secondary diagnostic locations\n- **setResourceMarkers()**: Updates the model with new marker data, computes diffs (added/removed/updated), and fires onDidChange event\n\n**Cache Management:**\n- Caches sorted resource list for performance\n- Clears cache on any update\n- Lazy-loads line splitting for marker messages\n\n---\n\n#### **Tree/Table Rendering**\n**Files:**\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersTreeViewer.ts` - Tree structure renderer\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersTable.ts` - Table structure renderer\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersFilterOptions.ts` - Filter UI\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markersFileDecorations.ts` - File decorations\n\n**Contribution:**\n**File:** `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/browser/markers.contribution.ts`\n\n**Purpose:** Registers the Problems view as a workbench panel and contributions.\n\n---\n\n#### **Markers Types & Constants**\n**Files:**\n- `/workspace/src/vs/platform/markers/common/markers.ts` - Public interfaces (IMarkerService, IMarkerData, MarkerSeverity)\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/contrib/markers/common/markers.ts` - View-specific constants\n\n---\n\n### **5. KEY PATTERNS & INSIGHTS**\n\n1. **Debouncing**: Both diagnostic changes (50ms) and marker service events use debouncing to batch updates efficiently\n\n2. **Event-Driven**: The entire system is event-driven with clear separation of concerns via service interfaces\n\n3. **Correlation IDs**: File watcher and marker services use correlation IDs to track related operations together\n\n4. **Dual Indexing**: MarkerService uses DoubleResourceMap to index by both resource and owner for efficient lookups\n\n5. **RPC Protocol**: Clear request/response separation between extension host and main thread with well-defined shapes\n\n6. **Filtering**: Multiple layers of filtering (unsupported schemas, file.exclude, severity levels, search text)\n\n7. **URI Normalization**: Extensive use of extUri for case-sensitive/insensitive comparisons across platforms\n\nThis architecture enables VS Code to efficiently handle thousands of diagnostics from multiple extensions while maintaining responsiveness through intelligent batching and filtering."}],"totalDurationMs":57171,"totalTokens":55980,"totalToolUseCount":36,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2767,"cache_read_input_tokens":49372,"output_tokens":3834,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2767,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

Files that no longer have errors still show old errors until manually opened.\n\n## Root Cause\n- Diagnostics are stored in `MarkerService` (central repository)\n- When files change on disk (git checkout), file system fires `onDidFilesChange` event\n- **No listener** exists to clear stale diagnostics when files change on disk\n- Diagnostics only refresh on in-editor text changes\n\n## Solution Architecture\n1. This allows extensions to re-analyze and set new diagnostics\n\n## Key Files\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.ts` - RPC handler for extension diagnostics\n- `/workspace/src/vs/platform/files/common/fileService.ts` - File system provider and change events\n- `/workspace/src/vs/platform/markers/common/markerService.ts` - Central diagnostic storage\n- `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/test/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.test.ts` - Tests\n\n## Implementation Steps\n1. '../../../platform/files/common/files.js';\n\n@extHostNamedCustomer(MainContext.MainThreadDiagnostics)\nexport class MainThreadDiagnostics implements MainThreadDiagnosticsShape {\n\n\tprivate readonly _activeOwners = new Set<string>();\n\n\tprivate readonly _proxy: ExtHostDiagnosticsShape;\n\tprivate readonly _markerListener: IDisposable;\n\tprivate readonly _fileListener: IDisposable;\n\n\tconstructor(\n\t\textHostContext: IExtHostContext,\n\t\t@IMarkerService private readonly _markerService: IMarkerService,\n\t\t@IUriIdentityService private readonly _uriIdentService: IUriIdentityService,\n\t\t@IFileService private readonly _fileService: IFileService,\n\t) {\n\t\tthis._proxy = extHostContext.getProxy(ExtHostContext.ExtHostDiagnostics);\n\n\t\tthis._markerListener = this._markerService.onMarkerChanged(this._forwardMarkers, this);\n\t\tthis._fileListener = this._fileService.onDidFilesChange(this._onDidFilesChange, this);\n\t}\n\n\tdispose(): void {\n\t\tthis._markerListener.dispose();\n\t\tthis._fileListener.dispose();\n\t\tthis._activeOwners.forEach(owner => this._markerService.changeAll(owner, []));\n\t\tthis._activeOwners.clear();\n\t}\n\n\tprivate _forwardMarkers(resources: readonly URI[]): void {\n\t\tconst data: [UriComponents, IMarkerData[]][] = [];\n\t\tfor (const resource of resources) {\n\t\t\tconst allMarkerData = this._markerService.read({ resource });\n\t\t\tif (allMarkerData.length === 0) {\n\t\t\t\tdata.push([resource, []]);\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tconst forgeinMarkerData = allMarkerData.filter(marker => !this._activeOwners.has(marker.owner));\n\t\t\t\tif (forgeinMarkerData.length > 0) {\n\t\t\t\t\tdata.push([resource, forgeinMarkerData]);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif (data.length > 0) {\n\t\t\tthis._proxy.$acceptMarkersChange(data);\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tprivate _onDidFilesChange(event: any): void {\n\t\t// Clear []) {\n\t\t\tfilesToClear.add(this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(resource).toString());\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Collect deleted files\n\t\tfor (const resource of event.rawDeleted ?? []) {\n\t\t\tfilesToClear.add(this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(resource).toString());\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Clear diagnostics for all affected files\n\t\tif (filesToClear.size > 0) {\n\t\t\tconst owners = new Set<string>();\n\t\t\tfor (const uriStr of filesToClear) {\n\t\t\t\tconst uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\n\t\t\t\tconst markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: uri });\n\t\t\t\tfor (const marker of markers) {\n\t\t\t\t\towners.add(marker.owner);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Clear all diagnostics for affected files and owners\n\t\t\tfor (const uriStr of filesToClear) {\n\t\t\t\tconst uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\n\t\t\t\tfor (const owner of owners) {\n\t\t\t\t\tthis._markerService.changeOne(owner, uri, []);\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t$changeMany(owner: string, entries: [UriComponents, IMarkerData[]][]): void {\n\t\tfor (const entry of entries) {\n\t\t\tconst [uri, markers] = entry;\n\t\t\tif (markers) {\n\t\t\t\tfor (const marker of markers) {\n\t\t\t\t\tif (marker.relatedInformation) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfor (const relatedInformation of marker.relatedInformation) {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\trelatedInformation.resource = URI.revive(relatedInformation.resource);\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif (marker.code && typeof marker.code !== 'string') {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tmarker.code.target = URI.revive(marker.code.target);\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tthis._markerService.changeOne(owner, this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(URI.revive(uri)), markers);\n\t\t}\n\t\tthis._activeOwners.add(owner);\n\t}\n\n\t$clear(owner: string): void {\n\t\tthis._markerService.changeAll(owner, []);\n\t\tthis._activeOwners.delete(owner);\n\t}\n}\n","numLines":120,"startLine":1,"totalLines":120}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"# Simplify: Code Review and Cleanup\n\nReview all changed files for reuse, quality, and efficiency. Fix any issues found.\n\n## Phase 1: Identify Changes\n\nRun `git diff` (or `git diff HEAD` if there are staged changes) to see what changed. If there are no git changes, review the most recently modified files that the user mentioned or that you edited earlier in this conversation.\n\n## Phase 2: Launch Three Review Agents in Parallel\n\nUse the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message. Pass each agent the full diff so it has the complete context.\n\n### Agent 1: Code Reuse Review\n\nFor each change:\n\n1. **Search for existing utilities and helpers** that could replace newly written code. Use Grep to find similar patterns elsewhere in the codebase — common locations are utility directories, shared modules, and files adjacent to the changed ones.\n2. **Flag any new function that duplicates existing functionality.** Suggest the existing function to use instead.\n3. **Flag any inline logic that could use an existing utility** — hand-rolled string manipulation, manual path handling, custom environment checks, ad-hoc type guards, and similar patterns are common candidates.\n\n### Agent 2: Code Quality Review\n\nReview the same changes for hacky patterns:\n\n1. **Redundant state**: state that duplicates existing state, cached values that could be derived, observers/effects that could be direct calls\n2. **Parameter sprawl**: adding new parameters to a function instead of generalizing or restructuring existing ones\n3. **Copy-paste with slight variation**: near-duplicate code blocks that should be unified with a shared abstraction\n4. **Leaky abstractions**: exposing internal details that should be encapsulated, or breaking existing abstraction boundaries\n5. **Stringly-typed code**: using raw strings where constants, enums (string unions), or branded types already exist in the codebase\n\n### Agent 3: Efficiency Review\n\nReview the same changes for efficiency:\n\n1. **Unnecessary work**: redundant computations, repeated file reads, duplicate network/API calls, N+1 patterns\n2. **Missed concurrency**: independent operations run sequentially when they could run in parallel\n3. **Hot-path bloat**: new blocking work added to startup or per-request/per-render hot paths\n4. **Unnecessary existence checks**: pre-checking file/resource existence before operating (TOCTOU anti-pattern) — operate directly and handle the error\n5. **Memory**: unbounded data structures, missing cleanup, event listener leaks\n6. **Overly broad operations**: reading entire files when only a portion is needed, loading all items when filtering for one\n\n## Phase 3: Fix Issues\n\nWait for all three agents to complete. Aggregate their findings and fix each issue directly. If a finding is a false positive or not worth addressing, note it and move on — do not argue with the finding, just skip it.\n\nWhen done, briefly summarize what was fixed (or confirm the code was already clean).\n\n\n## Additional Focus\n\n--path /workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.ts"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6badf5fd-141d-4b6c-ad07-2a2622d95059","uuid":"efa4423d-ff53-482e-b69f-8b82c8b04045","isSynthetic":true}

42→\tprivate _forwardMarkers(resources: readonly URI[]): void {\n    43→\t\tconst data: [UriComponents, IMarkerData[]][] = [];\n    44→\t\tfor (const resource of resources) {\n    45→\t\t\tconst 60→\tprivate _onDidFilesChange(event: any): void {\n    61→\t\t// Clear diagnostics for updated and deleted files to allow re-analysis\n    62→\t\tconst filesToClear = new Set<string>();\n    63→\n space for three dots\n   197→\t\t\tret = ret.setEndPosition(ret.startLineNumber, ret.startColumn + 2);\n   198→\t\t}\n   199→\n   200→\t\tret = model.validateRange(ret);\n   201→\n   202→\t\tif (maxColumn === 1 || ret.endColumn >= maxColumn) {\n   207→\t\t\t\t// empty line or behind eol\n   208→\t\t\t\t// keep the range as is, it will be rendered 1ch wide\n   209→\t\t\t\treturn ret;\n Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the code. Let me create a detailed performance analysis report.\n\n## Performance and Efficiency Issues Analysis\n\nI've reviewed the `_onDidFilesChange` method in `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.ts` (lines 60-93) and identified several significant efficiency issues:\n\n### Critical Performance Issues\n\n#### 1. **Double String-to-URI Conversion (N+1 Pattern)**\n**Severity: HIGH**\n\nThe code converts URI strings back to URI objects twice, doubling the parsing work:\n\n```typescript\n// First loop: String -> URI (line 78)\nconst uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\nconst markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: uri });\n\n// Second loop: String -> URI again (line 87)\nconst uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\nfor (const owner of owners) {\n    this._markerService.changeOne(owner, uri, []);\n}\n```\n\n**Impact**: If there are 100 changed files and 5 owners, this performs 200 `URI.parse()` operations instead of 100.\n\n**Recommendation**: Store URI objects instead of strings:\n```typescript\nconst filesToClear = new Map<string, URI>(); // Map string keys to URI objects\n\nfor (const resource of event.rawUpdated ?? []) {\n    const uri = this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(resource);\n    filesToClear.set(uri.toString(), uri);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n#### 2. **Two-Pass Algorithm (O(n*m) Nested Loop)**\n**Severity: HIGH**\n\nThe code iterates over `filesToClear` twice:\n- **First pass** (lines 77-83): Collects all unique owner names\n- **Second pass** (lines 86-91): Clears markers for each (owner, file) combination\n\nThis creates an O(n*m) pattern where `n` = files and `m` = unique owners.\n\n**Example scenario**: 100 changed files with 10 unique diagnostic owners = 1,000 `changeOne()` calls instead of the optimal 100.\n\n**Recommendation**: Combine into a single pass structure using a Map:\n```typescript\nconst filesToClearWithOwners = new Map<string, Set<string>>();\n// First pass: collect owners per file\nfor (const [uriStr, uri] of filesToClear) {\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: uri });\n    const owners = new Set(markers.map(m => m.owner));\n    filesToClearWithOwners.set(uriStr, owners);\n}\n\n// Second pass: clear exactly what's needed\nfor (const [uriStr, owners] of filesToClearWithOwners) {\n    const uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\n    for (const owner of owners) {\n        this._markerService.changeOne(owner, uri, []);\n    }\n}\n```\n\nWhile this still has the loop structure, it at least collects data efficiently.\n\n---\n\n#### 3. **Unnecessary Iteration Over Diagnostic Owners**\n**Severity: MEDIUM**\n\nThe code collects ALL unique owners across all files, then clears diagnostics for EVERY owner on EVERY file, even if that owner never had diagnostics on that file:\n\n```typescript\n// Collects owners globally\nconst owners = new Set<string>();\nfor (const uriStr of filesToClear) {\n    const uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: uri });\n    for (const marker of markers) {\n        owners.add(marker.owner);  // Global set\n    }\n}\n\n// Then clears for ALL owners on ALL files\nfor (const uriStr of filesToClear) {\n    const uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\n    for (const owner of owners) {  // Every owner for every file!\n        this._markerService.changeOne(owner, uri, []);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Better approach**: Clear only the owners that actually have diagnostics on each file (per-file basis):\n\n```typescript\nfor (const uri of filesToClear.values()) {\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: uri });\n    const owners = new Set(markers.map(m => m.owner));\n    for (const owner of owners) {\n        this._markerService.changeOne(owner, uri, []);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n#### 4. **Redundant Canonical URI Conversion in Data Collection**\n**Severity: MEDIUM**\n\nThe code calls `asCanonicalUri()` during initial collection but then converts back to strings unnecessarily:\n\n```typescript\n// Line 65-66: Convert to canonical, then to string\nfilesToClear.add(this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(resource).toString());\n\n// Line 78-79: Parse string back to URI (loses canonical form)\nconst uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\n```\n\nThe string conversion loses the normalized form, potentially causing the `URI.parse()` to create a non-canonical URI.\n\n**Recommendation**: Store URI objects directly:\n```typescript\nconst filesToClear = new Set<URI>();\nfor (const resource of event.rawUpdated ?? []) {\n    filesToClear.add(this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(resource));\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Summary of Recommendations\n\n| Issue | Current | Optimized |\n|-------|---------|-----------|\n| URI parsing | 2n (parse twice per file) | n (parse once per file) |\n| MarkerService.read() calls | n (per file in first loop) | n (same, unavoidable) |\n| changeOne() calls | n*m (every owner on every file) | Σ owners per file (only affected) |\n| Data structure conversions | URI → String → URI | URI (direct) |\n\n**High-impact optimization**: Change from collecting global owners to per-file owners, reducing the second loop from `n*m` to `Σ(owners_per_file_i)`, which is typically much smaller."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a109f99cb25a3469c (for resuming to continue this agent's work if needed)\n<usage>total_tokens: 22761\ntool_uses: 6\nduration_ms: 22708</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6badf5fd-141d-4b6c-ad07-2a2622d95059","uuid":"cf4d0157-4801-42c9-8865-f82676466772","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review this git diff for efficiency and performance issues:\n\n```diff\n+\tprivate _onDidFilesChange(event: any): void {\n+\t\t// Clear diagnostics for updated and deleted files to allow re-analysis\n+\t\tconst filesToClear = new Set<string>();\n+\n+\t\t// Collect updated files\n+\t\tfor (const resource of event.rawUpdated ?? Let me create a detailed performance analysis report.\n\n## Performance and Efficiency Issues Analysis\n\nI've reviewed the `_onDidFilesChange` method in `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.ts` (lines 60-93) and identified several significant efficiency issues:\n\n### Critical Performance Issues\n\n#### 1. **Double String-to-URI Conversion (N+1 Pattern)**\n**Severity: HIGH**\n\nThe code converts URI strings back to URI objects twice, doubling the parsing work:\n\n```typescript\n// First loop: String -> URI (line 78)\nconst uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\nconst markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: uri });\n\n// Second loop: String -> URI again (line 87)\nconst uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\nfor (const owner of owners) {\n    this._markerService.changeOne(owner, uri, []);\n}\n```\n\n**Impact**: If there are 100 changed files and 5 owners, this performs 200 `URI.parse()` operations instead of 100.\n\n**Recommendation**: Store URI objects instead of strings:\n```typescript\nconst filesToClear = new Map<string, URI>(); // Map string keys to URI objects\n\nfor (const resource of event.rawUpdated ?? []) {\n    const uri = this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(resource);\n    filesToClear.set(uri.toString(), uri);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n#### 2. **Two-Pass Algorithm (O(n*m) Nested Loop)**\n**Severity: HIGH**\n\nThe code iterates over `filesToClear` twice:\n- **First pass** (lines 77-83): Collects all unique owner names\n- **Second pass** (lines 86-91): Clears markers for each (owner, file) combination\n\nThis creates an O(n*m) pattern where `n` = files and `m` = unique owners.\n\n**Example scenario**: 100 changed files with 10 unique diagnostic owners = 1,000 `changeOne()` calls instead of the optimal 100.\n\n**Recommendation**: Combine into a single pass structure using a Map:\n```typescript\nconst filesToClearWithOwners = new Map<string, Set<string>>();\n// First pass: collect owners per file\nfor (const [uriStr, uri] of filesToClear) {\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: uri });\n    const owners = new Set(markers.map(m => m.owner));\n    filesToClearWithOwners.set(uriStr, owners);\n}\n\n// Second pass: clear exactly what's needed\nfor (const [uriStr, owners] of filesToClearWithOwners) {\n    const uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\n    for (const owner of owners) {\n        this._markerService.changeOne(owner, uri, []);\n    }\n}\n```\n\nWhile this still has the loop structure, it at least collects data efficiently.\n\n---\n\n#### 3. **Unnecessary Iteration Over Diagnostic Owners**\n**Severity: MEDIUM**\n\nThe code collects ALL unique owners across all files, then clears diagnostics for EVERY owner on EVERY file, even if that owner never had diagnostics on that file:\n\n```typescript\n// Collects owners globally\nconst owners = new Set<string>();\nfor (const uriStr of filesToClear) {\n    const uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: uri });\n    for (const marker of markers) {\n        owners.add(marker.owner);  // Global set\n    }\n}\n\n// Then clears for ALL owners on ALL files\nfor (const uriStr of filesToClear) {\n    const uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\n    for (const owner of owners) {  // Every owner for every file!\n        this._markerService.changeOne(owner, uri, []);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Better approach**: Clear only the owners that actually have diagnostics on each file (per-file basis):\n\n```typescript\nfor (const uri of filesToClear.values()) {\n    const markers = this._markerService.read({ resource: uri });\n    const owners = new Set(markers.map(m => m.owner));\n    for (const owner of owners) {\n        this._markerService.changeOne(owner, uri, []);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n#### 4. **Redundant Canonical URI Conversion in Data Collection**\n**Severity: MEDIUM**\n\nThe code calls `asCanonicalUri()` during initial collection but then converts back to strings unnecessarily:\n\n```typescript\n// Line 65-66: Convert to canonical, then to string\nfilesToClear.add(this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(resource).toString());\n\n// Line 78-79: Parse string back to URI (loses canonical form)\nconst uri = URI.parse(uriStr);\n```\n\nThe string conversion loses the normalized form, potentially causing the `URI.parse()` to create a non-canonical URI.\n\n**Recommendation**: Store URI objects directly:\n```typescript\nconst filesToClear = new Set<URI>();\nfor (const resource of event.rawUpdated ?? []) {\n    filesToClear.add(this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(resource));\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Summary of Recommendations\n\n| Issue | Current | Optimized |\n|-------|---------|-----------|\n| URI parsing | 2n (parse twice per file) | n (parse once per file) |\n| MarkerService.read() calls | n (per file in first loop) | n (same, unavoidable) |\n| changeOne() calls | n*m (every owner on every file) | Σ owners per file (only affected) |\n| Data structure conversions | URI → String → URI | URI (direct) |\n\n**High-impact optimization**: Change from collecting global owners to per-file owners, reducing the second loop from `n*m` to `Σ(owners_per_file_i)`, which is typically much smaller."}],"totalDurationMs":22708,"totalTokens":22761,"totalToolUseCount":6,"usage":{"input_tokens":6,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3721,"cache_read_input_tokens":17566,"output_tokens":1468,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3721,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

68→\n    69→\t\t// Collect deleted files\n    70→\t\tfor (const resource of event.rawDeleted) {\n    71→\t\t\tfilesToClear.add(this._uriIdentService.asCanonicalUri(resource));\n    72→\t\t}\n    73→\n 104→\t$clear(owner: string): void {\n   105→\t\tthis._markerService.changeAll(owner, []);\n   106→\t\tthis._activeOwners.delete(owner);\n   107→\t}\n   108→}\n   109→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever resources) {\n\t\t\tconst allMarkerData = this._markerService.read({ resource });\n\t\t\tif (allMarkerData.length === 0) {\n\t\t\t\tdata.push([resource, []]);\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tconst Files that no longer have errors still show old errors until manually opened.\n\n## Root Cause\n- Diagnostics are stored in `MarkerService` (central repository)\n- When files change on disk (git checkout), file system fires `onDidFilesChange` event\n- **No listener** exists to clear stale diagnostics when files change on disk\n- Diagnostics only refresh on in-editor text changes\n\n## Solution Architecture\n1. Files that no longer have errors still show old errors until manually opened.\n\n## Root Cause\n- Diagnostics are stored in `MarkerService` (central repository)\n- When files change on disk (git checkout), file system fires `onDidFilesChange` event\n- **No listener** exists to clear stale diagnostics when files change on disk\n- Diagnostics only refresh on in-editor text changes\n\n## Solution Implemented\n1. ✅ Added file system change listener to `MainThreadDiagnostics`\n2. ✅ Type-safe event handling with `FileChangesEvent` parameter\n3. ✅ Single-pass algorithm: clear only diagnostics that actually exist\n4. ✅ No unnecessary string/URI conversions\n5. ✅ Added tests for file update and deletion scenarios\n\n## Key Changes Made\n- File: `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.ts`\n  - Stores URIs directly in Set<URI> (no string conversions)\n\n- File: `/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/test/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.test.ts`\n  - Added two new tests: `clear diagnostics on file changes` and `clear diagnostics on file deletion`\n  - Here's what was completed:\n\n### **Implementation Overview**\n\nThe fix adds a file system change listener to `MainThreadDiagnostics` that clears stale diagnostics when files change on disk, allowing language servers to re-analyze and provide updated diagnostics.\n\n### **Key Changes**\n\n1. **`/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.ts`**\n   - Injected `IFileService` dependency\n   - Subscribed to `onDidFilesChange` events in constructor\n   - Implemented `_onDidFilesChange()` method to clear diagnostics for updated/deleted files\n   - Proper resource cleanup in `dispose()` method\n\n2. **`/workspace/src/vs/workbench/api/test/browser/mainThreadDiagnostics.test.ts`**\n   - Updated all 5 `MainThreadDiagnostics` instantiations to provide `IFileService` mock\n   - Added 2 new tests: `clear diagnostics on file changes` and `clear diagnostics on file deletion`\n\n### **Code Review Improvements Applied**\n\n✅ **Type Safety**: Changed `event: any` to `event: FileChangesEvent` for compile-time validation\n\n✅ **Performance**: Reduced from O(n*m) two-pass to single-pass O(n + Σmarkers) algorithm\n\n✅ **Memory**: Eliminated redundant URI.parse()/toString() conversions\n\n✅ **Clarity**: Only clears diagnostics that actually exist for each file\n\n✅ **Code Quality**: Reduced method from 37 to 23 lines (36% reduction)\n\n### **How It Works**\n\n1. When files change on disk (git checkout), `IFileService` fires `onDidFilesChange`\n2. The new handler collects updated and deleted files\n3.
